title: Romeo and Juliet
pairs: What genre is Romeo and Juliet?
Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy by William Shakespeare.
What kind of play is Romeo and Juliet?
Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy.
Did Shakespeare write Romeo and Juliet?
Yes, Romeo and Juliet was written by William Shakespeare.
Where is Romeo and Juliet set?
Romeo and Juliet is set in Verona, Italy.
What is the setting of Romeo and Juliet?
The setting of Romeo and Juliet is Verona, Italy.
In what location does Romeo and Juliet take place?
Romeo and Juliet takes place in Verona, Italy.
Who is Romeo in Romeo and Juliet?
Romeo is a young man from the Montague family who falls in love with Juliet.

Romeo is a young man from the Montague family who falls in love with Juliet.

What are the main themes of Romeo and Juliet?
The main themes of Romeo and Juliet include love, fate, family conflict, youth and impulsiveness, and death.
What does Romeo and Juliet explore?
Romeo and Juliet explores themes of love, fate, family conflict, youth and impulsiveness, and death.
Is love a theme in Romeo and Juliet?
Yes, love is a central theme in Romeo and Juliet.
Is fate a theme in Romeo and Juliet?
Yes, fate is a central theme in Romeo and Juliet.
Is family conflict a theme in Romeo and Juliet?
Yes, family conflict is a central theme in Romeo and Juliet.
Why can Romeo and Juliet not be together openly?
Their families, the Montagues and the Capulets, are bitter enemies locked in an ancient feud.
How do Romeo and Juliet meet?
They meet at a Capulet party, not knowing each other's family name.
Who kills Tybalt?
Romeo kills Tybalt after Tybalt kills Mercutio.
What is Friar Lawrence's plan?
Friar Lawrence gives Juliet a potion to fake her death so she can escape with Romeo.
Why does Romeo kill himself?
Romeo finds Juliet apparently dead in the tomb and kills himself with poison in despair.
How does Juliet die?
Juliet wakes to find Romeo dead and kills herself with his dagger.
What ends the feud between the families?
The deaths of Romeo and Juliet reconcile the Montague and Capulet families.
How does Romeo and Juliet end?
Both Romeo and Juliet die in the tomb; their deaths end the feud between their families.
What is the ending of Romeo and Juliet?
Both Romeo and Juliet die in the tomb; their deaths end the feud between their families.
Why is Romeo and Juliet a tragedy?
Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy because both romeo and juliet die in the tomb; their deaths end the feud between their families.
Who says "What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet." in Romeo and Juliet?
Juliet says this famous line in Romeo and Juliet.
What is a famous line from Romeo and Juliet?
A famous line from Romeo and Juliet is: "What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet."
